steps.title: Identify the key detail to illustrate
steps.content: The passage describes Lost in the Shuffle by specifying that two young men are depicted floating in the bottom left and upper right corners of the canvas. The correct answer must convey that precise spatial detail.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Evaluate each answer choice against that requirement:
  • Choice A — At 36 by 24 inches, Harper's Lost in the Shuffle is even larger than the sizable 24-by-30-inch painting Lightness of Being.: This addresses the painting’s physical dimensions, not the placement of figures within it. ✗
  • Choice B — Harper captures the water in paintings such as Lightness of Being and Lost in the Shuffle in vivid hues of sky blue and indigo.: This describes color palette across multiple paintings, not the composition of Lost in the Shuffle specifically. ✗
  • Choice C — In Harper's painting Lost in the Shuffle, two young men are depicted in the bottom left and upper right corners of the canvas.: This directly conveys the exact spatial placement of the two figures described in the passage. ✓
  • Choice D — While the number of figures may differ, constant among Harper's hyperrealistic works is the calm mood that the paintings evoke.: This discusses mood across Harper’s broader body of work and says nothing about where figures appear in Lost in the Shuffle. ✗
That is the correct answer.
